Went in on 2/8 for a simple oil change. Upon checkout I was bombarded by two employees  insisting that my battery was not functioning at full capacity and that my brakes and rotors were warped. I declined the service and said I would deal with it at another time. My car was not giving any indication of issues. The employees would not let it go and continued to pressure me pointing out that the car was unsafe. I still declined and took the car for a second opinion. The second mechanic to check out my brakes/rotors and battery made it very clear that there was nothing wrong with either. He explained that I have AT MINIMUM 60% left to the brakes and my battery was just fine. I find Mavis in Lakewood to be untrustworthy beyond an oil change.

I was on a bachelorette trip in Seaside Heights, and the morning we were supposed to check out and hit the road back to southeastern Virginia, I discovered a giant nail in my tire and it was completely flat. Not exactly the kind of sendoff we were hoping for. Womp womp.

One of my friends started calling around to find a tire shop that could help us out quickly (and on a Sunday morning), and after several dead ends, she reached Jessica at this location. Jessica told us to come on in and assured us they’d take care of it. So we drove the 30 minutes over and she absolutely followed through on that promise.

The team got right to work the moment we arrived. Unfortunately, the nail was in a spot that couldn’t be safely plugged, but Jessica walked me through my options and helped me pick out new tires (which even came with a rebate). They got everything done fast - we were in and out in under an hour!

Huge thanks to Jessica, Tom, and the crew in the shop. They turned a stressful situation into a smooth experience and got us safely back on the road to Virginia. Definitely recommend if you ever find yourself in a pinch in the area!

Its a shame. Mavis has very competetive prices on tires, and in the past I've had a good exprience with them. Yesterday when the mechanic Jian recommended more services than expected he was willing to accomodate me somehwhat to meet my budget. We agreed on a price that I thought was fair and he finished the job. When I got the invoice I noticed that the original $25 fee that is only meant as a hold until you bring the car in (the guy on the phone specifically told me when I booked the service, that this fee is refunded as soon as you bring the car in), was included in the bill! That is instead of being charged the amount we agreed on, they inlcuded the $25 fee in the new invoice. I thought it was very sneaky of them. I called them up, I thought maybe there was a misunderstanding, but the manager was adamant that I didnt "understand" how the fee works. Load of BS. I really wish they would be more commited to their customers, and show some professionalism, and not doctor invoices like that. It's a shame, because otherwise I would have been a happy customer. I’ve used them many times for tires, brakes, repairs, etc. so l brought my van to Mavis in Lakewood yesterday because my battery light came on. Ed the mechanic who I’ve dealt with more than once determined the battery needed to be replaced. He replaced it but could not get the battery light on my dash to turn off. I had to leave for work so I went back this morning to get it turned off. Unfortunately at that point Gian the Store Manager, gave me the news that my alternator was also bad. Of course I was not happy, especially once I saw the cost for a new replacement. But thankfully Ed remained patient with me and was able to give me a better price by suggesting a rebuilt alt. He ordered it and I brought my van back around noon. I received a phone call from Ed about an hour later. Expecting more bad news I was unexpectedly yet pleasantly surprised. He told me the alternator was not the problem. They had determined that the cable connecting the battery to the alternator was the problem. I just went back to pick up my van and all seems to be working. I only paid for the battery from yesterday. Today’s stint at their shop cost me zero. The people here are honest and pleasant. For someone like me who knows nothing about automotive mechanical issues, it’s truly appreciated. I highly recommend Mavis on Rt 70 in Lakewood to anyone who needs auto repairs. Mavis you’ve respired my faith in humanity. Thank you. Joe
